text:
Responsible Party (RP): A person or business responsible for a hazardous material or site; whenever possible, the EPA requires responsible parties to make certain notifications of releases and to clean up the sites they have contaminated. Standard Operational Guideline (sog): A written checklist or instructions that spell out general steps needed to accomplish a task. Standard Operational Procedure (sop): A written checklist or set of instructions, having the force of a directive, that spells out the steps needed to accomplish a task. Threshold Planning Quantity (TPQ): A quantity designated for each chemical on the EHS list. Any facility having EHS on hand at or above the TPQ is required to notify AZSERC, the CCLEPC, and the local Fire Department/District having jurisdiction over the area where the facility is located that such facility is subject to the emergency planning requirements of SARA Title III and ARS Title 49.. Toxicity: The ability of a substance to cause damage to living tissue, impairment of the central nervous system, severe illness, or death when ingested, inhaled, or absorbed through the skin. Vulnerable Zone: An area over which the airborne concentration of a chemical involved in an accidental release could reach the level of concern. Vulnerability Analysis: Assessment of elements in the community that are subject to damage should a hazardous materials release occur. The analysis includes gathering information about the extent of the vulnerable zone, conditions that influence the zone, the size and type of the population within the zone, private and public property that might be damaged, and the environment that might be affected. Weapons of Mass Destruction (wmd): Weapons of Mass Destruction are characterized in five major areas, which include chemical, biological, radiological, nuclear, and explosive weapons (CBRNE). wmD is defined as any weapon or device that is intended or can cause death or serious bodily injury to a significant number of people through the release, dissemination, or impact of toxic or poisonous chemicals or their precursors, a disease organism, or radiation/radioactivity. 1. Roll Call - Members of the Cochise County Local Emergency Planning Committee will attend either
in person or by telephone, video or internet conferencing. Supervisor Crosby, present; Mr. Venos, absent, excused; Mr. Higgins, present; Mr. Fullen, present; Mr.. Steinberg, present; Mr. Noland, present; Mr. Scott, absent; Mr. Allen, present. Mr. Fry, present..

section: ATTACHMENT F > Cochise County Local Emergency Planning Committee (LEPC)
text:
4.Approval of the meeting minutes of the March 20, 2025, LEPC Meeting..
Supervisor Crosby moved to approve the minutes of the March 20, 2025, LEPC meeting; Mr. Noland
seconded, and the motion passed unanimously..
5. Presentation, discussion, and adoption of the Local Emergency Planning Committee Hazardous
Materials Response Plan.
Mr. Duchon presented on the proposed changes to the Hazardous Materials Response Plan, which
included: formatting in section 3.1 and 7.1; adding HAzMAT Technician availability to section 3.1; adding a.
reference to the County Emergency Operations Plan, Annex 9 to section 4.7; adding a HAzMAT Size Up
Guide as Attachment G; and proposed changes to Attachment B regarding HAzMAT Technician availability.
Supervisor Crosby asked about the Emergency Management Resolution and Policy which the Board of
Supervisors recently discussed during a work session, and he recommended the Board restrict its authority.
Mr. Fullen asked about the Sunsites-Pearce Fire District Inventory being from 2024. Mr. Duchon stated he.
would revisit that to update it. Supervisor Crosby referenced a recent Board work session regarding County
maintained roads and using quantity of residents, emergency response, and school traffic as criteria. Mr.
Steinberg noticed a typo in the Sunsites-Pearce FD inventory..
Mr. Noland moved to approve the plan; Supervisor Crosby seconded, and the motion passed unanimously.
6. Presentation, discussion and possible recommendations regarding Special Event Permit for
Weekend Camping Event (aka Decompression Event) on October 10 to 12, 2025.
Mr. Duchon presented the special event permit and noted the LEPc had reviewed this item before several
times; this event was smaller than the spring event with only 200-300 persons; and there would be no
effigy burning. Mr. Taylor, from Development Services Department discussed the conditions, such as.
staying out of the floodplain; and referenced recent improvements made to roadway.
Ms. Mica Harkins, the permit applicant, stated there would be no amplified sound, they would use a burn.
barrel, no large fires, and attendance would be closer to 150..
Mr. Steinberg stated he had no recommendations for the special event permit, Supervisor Crosby agreed,
Mr. Higgins concurred, Mr. Fry agreed, Mr. Noland agreed, and Mr. Fullen agreed.

7. Next Meeting Date/Location: Thursday, September 18, 2025, 1:30 pm, at the Sierra Vista Public
Library, Conference Room, 2600 East Tacoma Street, Sierra Vista, Arizona.. Mr. Steinberg stated the next meeting would be on Thursday, September 18, 2025, at 1:30 pm, at the. Sierra Vista Public Library. 8.Meeting Adjournment Mr. Steinberg adjourned the meeting at 1:53 pm..
